response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack engineer with a broad background in TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web/mobile systems, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments writing components for a C IDE and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years of Windows-platform C++ required. He has no fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge, and his financial-sector exposure (RIDE Capital) was TypeScript/GraphQL work unrelated to capital-markets systems. SQL is listed as a skill but there is no evidence of depth with SQL Server or Oracle.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software engineering across diverse domains demonstrates strong foundational engineering instincts and adaptability
Experience at Sociomantic with low-latency, high-throughput micro-services shows familiarity with performance-critical systems thinking
Demonstrated engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and code-quality practices across multiple roles
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months professionally at National Instruments plus a thesis project) with no evidence of sustained Windows-platform C++ development
Zero fixed income, derivatives, investment banking, or capital markets domain knowledge anywhere in the resume
Primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/MongoDB/DLang with no mention of SQL Server, Oracle, STL, Python, Kafka, or Summit — nearly all mandatory and desirable skills are absent
